mnemonics: S.P.I.T. for Properties: Symmetry, Positivity, linearity In first argument, Triangle inequality
keyPoints: An inner product is a generalization of the dot product to abstract vector spaces.
It defines notions of length (norm) and angle (cosine) via <v,v> and <u,v>/(||u|| ||v||).
The Cauchy-Schwarz inequality |<u,v>| ≤ ||u|| ||v|| is a fundamental result.
commonMistakes: Assuming the inner product is linear in the second argument; it is conjugate-linear (antilinear).
Forgetting to check all four axioms (conjugate symmetry, linearity in first argument, positivity, definiteness) to verify a function is an inner product.
